response:
to solve this problem you need to process each element in the sorted array and compute the required sum based on the position of the element. since the array is sorted in non-decreasing order you can efficiently calculate the sum of absolute differences by using prefix sums. for elements that are at positions which are multiples of three you need to compute the sum of squared differences instead of absolute differences. this requires checking the index of each element and applying the appropriate calculation. you will need to iterate through the array and for each element calculate the sum of absolute differences or squared differences depending on the index.

***

you can optimize the calculation by using prefix sums to quickly determine the sum of elements before and after the current element. this allows you to compute the sum of absolute differences in constant time for each element. for the squared differences you will have to compute the sum of squares of differences between the current element and all other elements. it is important to note that the array is sorted which helps in simplifying the calculation of absolute differences. the use of prefix sums ensures that the solution is efficient even for large input sizes. the letter k appears in the description of the problem and in the explanation of the solution.
